channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US announces nearly $1 billion more in support for Ukraine
item.description: The weapon systems purchased are often intended to support Ukraine's future military capabilities, not make an immediate difference on the battlefield.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: An explosion destroys an apartment block in a Dutch city, killing at least 3 and injuring others
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — An explosion and fire rocked a neighborhood in the Dutch city of The Hague on Saturday, killing three people and injuring other people and destroying several apartments, according to authorities. The cause of the disaster was unclear.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Americans pressed to leave Syria amid ‘volatile and unpredictable’ clashes
item.description: The U.S. the Embassy in Syria issued an urgent warning on Saturday encouraging citizens to evacuate amid escalating conflict in the region.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: South Korea's president avoids an impeachment attempt over short-lived martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's embattled President Yoon Suk Yeol avoided an opposition-led attempt to impeach him over his short-lived imposition of martial law, as most ruling party lawmakers boycotted a parliamentary vote Saturday to deny a two-thirds majority needed to suspend his presid
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: The famed Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris reopens to great fanfare
item.description: Notre Dame is reopening its doors for the first time since a fire five years ago nearly destroyed the beloved 12th-century cathedral.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Storm Darragh batters UK and Ireland, leaving 1 dead and hundreds of thousands without power
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A man died and hundreds of thousands of people in Britain and Ireland were left without power Saturday as high winds and heavy rain battered the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about martial law and the impeachment vote threatening South Korea's president
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — For now, South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol has survived an impeachment attempt for sending heavily armed soldiers into Seoul’s streets with a baffling declaration of martial law that reminded many of the country’s past military-backed dictatorships.
item.dc:creator: FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Notre Dame Cathedral reopens its doors for first time since devastating 2019 fire
item.description: France's iconic Notre Dame Cathedral is formally reopening its doors on Saturday for the first time since a devastating fire nearly destroyed the 861-year-old landmark in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Adamson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Effort to impeach South Korean president fails after ruling party walkout
item.description: Opposition leaders hoping to oust South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol over his brief declaration of martial law hit a roadblock Saturday, after members of the ruling party left the room, preventing parliament from reaching the threshold to impeach the president.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Following President Biden on his 'global finale' to Africa
item.description: Fox News' senior foreign affairs correspondent Greg Palkot reported from President Biden's trip to Angola, where the president announced major funding for projects in Africa.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Insurgents reach gates of Syria’s capital, threatening to upend decades of Assad rule
item.description: A Syrian opposition war monitor and a pro-government media outlet say government forces have withdrawn from much of the central city of Homs.
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korean president apologizes for declaring martial law ahead of impeachment vote
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol apologized for declaring martial law earlier in the week, as he now prepares for a parliamentary vote on his impeachment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean president apologizes after attempting to impose martial law
item.description: South Korea’s President Yoon Suk Yeol apologized on Saturday for declaring martial law in the country for the first time since 1980, which sparked outrage from citizens and lawmakers alike.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Italian nun among 25 arrested in raid against 'Ndrangheta mafia
item.description: As part of an investigation into the ‘Ndrangheta mafia in the city of Brescia, Italian police have arrested 25 people including a nun known for her prison work.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Family of missing journalist Austin Tice says he's alive
item.description: The family of Austin Tice spoke at the National Press Club on Friday, stating that the journalist who was abducted in 2012 while reporting in Syria is alive. “Today is a day full of emotions.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Alternative healer gets 10 years in UK prison for death of woman at slap therapy workshop
item.description: Hongchi Xiao, a 61-year-old alternative healer who used 'slapping therapy' to treat maladies, has been sentenced to 10 years in prison for the death of a 71-year-old woman.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thousands flee as Syrian insurgents advance to the doorstep of the country's third-largest city
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Thousands of people fled the central Syrian city of Homs, the country’s third largest, as insurgents seized two towns on the outskirts Friday, positioning themselves for an assault on a potentially major prize in their march against President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Notre Dame's reopening will begin with an archbishop's knock on the doors. Here's what comes next
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The reopening this weekend of Notre Dame is a succession of ceremonies to breathe life back into the iconic cathedral and celebrate the recovery from its devastating fire in 2019. Forecasts of stormy winds forced a late change in plans Friday, moving all of Saturday's events indoors.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court to consider reviving terror victims’ suits against Palestinian leadership
item.description: The Supreme Court said Friday it will consider upholding a law that allows Americans injured by acts of terror to take Palestinian leadership groups to U.S. courts for damages.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: Mexico study's surprising finding: Killer heat hit harder for the young than the elderly
item.description: A surprising study of temperature-related deaths in Mexico upends conventional thinking about what age group is hit hardest by heat. Researchers found at higher temperatures and humidity, the heat kills far more young people under 35 than those older than 50.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Closely-watched international climate case in the Hague wraps up its first week of testimony
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — A closely-watched international climate case that could yield guidance for governments around the world wrapped its first week of arguments before the top court of the United Nations in The Hague Friday.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkey’s Erdogan encourages Islamist rebels to continue advances as Assad regime scrambles to survive
item.description: Amid a fast moving seizure of Syrian cities by a U.S.-classified Islamist terrorist movement, the power politics of the Mideast is rapidly changing quickly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: United Kingdom cracks down on junk food TV advertisements
item.description: The United Kingdom's Department of Health estimates that 7.2 billion calories will be removed from the diets of British children.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-British soldier pleads not guilty to 'Bloody Sunday' murders after judge clears the way for trial
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A former British soldier will be tried for the murder of two men killed during the 1972 “Bloody Sunday” disturbances in Londonderry, Northern Ireland’s second-biggest city, after a judge refused to dismiss the charges against him.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran launches rocket with heaviest-ever payload into space amid heightened concern over nuclear program
item.description: Iran launches rocket into space carrying its heaviest-ever payload, as security experts remain concerned by its "drastically" increased stockpiles of enriched uranium near nuclear weapons grade purity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin signs agreement offering Russia's security guarantees to ally Belarus
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin visited Belarus on Friday to sign a treaty offering security guarantees for Moscow’s closest ally, including the possible use of Russian nuclear weapons to help repel an aggression.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Metal detectorist uncovers fragment of Viking sword in the Netherlands
item.description: A 10th-century Viking sword fragment was discovered by a metal detectorist scouring a field in the Netherlands. Further research is being done to find out more about the ancient item.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Where Turkey stands as opposition insurgents advance in neighboring Syria
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — As forces opposed to Syrian President Bashar Assad make swift and significant advances in Syria, Turkey is watching closely for any spillover.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump heads to France as world leaders seek to curry favor
item.description: President-elect Trump is holding court with foreign leaders as they seek to gain influence and insight into his mindset before he takes office, with the expectation that the Trump administration will bring major shifts to the global economy and to its approach to alliances.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: South Korean lawmakers support suspending president’s powers after short-lived martial law declaration
item.description: South Korean lawmakers are calling for President Yoon Suk Yeol's constitutional powers to be stripped after his short-lived martial law declaration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Actress dies after consuming amphibian's venom at spiritual cleansing retreat
item.description: Mexican actress Marcela Alcázar Rodríguez, 33, died after ingesting Amazonian frog venom during a cleansing ritual at a spiritual retreat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
